hero.description: Create beautiful Bismillah (بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم) calligraphy in 11 authentic Arabic styles including Thuluth, Diwani, Kufic, and Naskh. Download free as PNG, SVG, or JPG — no sign-up.

sections.intro: Bismillah (بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم) can be rendered in every major Arabic calligraphy tradition. The style you choose changes both the visual character and the appropriate context — Thuluth carries ceremonial weight, Kufic gives a modern architectural feel, Diwani is decorative and courtly, and Naskh is clean and readable. Try each style in the free Bismillah generator above and download your favorite as a PNG or SVG.

h3s.title: Thuluth Bismillah

image.alt: Bismillah rendered in Thuluth Arabic calligraphy style on aged parchment

h3s.content: Thuluth is the most historically revered style for Bismillah calligraphy, used extensively in Quranic manuscripts and mosque decoration since the 11th century. Its curved, elongated letters give Bismillah a sense of formal grace. Select "Thuluth (Scheherazade)" in the generator above to render Bismillah in this classic script.

h3s.title: Diwani Bismillah

image.alt: Bismillah rendered in Diwani Arabic calligraphy style with flowing ornate curves in burgundy

h3s.content: Diwani produces flowing, ornate Bismillah calligraphy originally developed by Ottoman court scribes for royal decrees. Its curves and ligatures create a decorative, artistic look ideal for wedding invitations, greeting cards, and gift prints. Choose "Diwani (Aref Ruqaa)" in the font dropdown to generate Bismillah in Diwani style.

h3s.title: Kufic Bismillah

image.alt: Bismillah rendered in Kufic Arabic calligraphy with bold geometric letterforms on white

h3s.content: Kufic is the oldest Arabic calligraphy style, with angular geometric letterforms perfectly suited to modern minimalist Bismillah designs. It works especially well for wall art, tattoo references, and Islamic architectural signage. Select "Kufi (Reem Kufi)" or "Noto Kufi Arabic" in the generator to render Bismillah in Kufic script.

h3s.title: Naskh Bismillah

image.alt: Bismillah rendered in Naskh Arabic calligraphy style with clean readable green letters

h3s.content: Naskh offers a clean, highly legible Bismillah calligraphy — this is the style used in modern Quran printings and everyday Arabic text. It is the safest choice when clarity matters more than decoration, such as educational materials or accessible signage. Choose "Naskh (Amiri)" in the font selector above.

sections.h2: Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim in Arabic Calligraphy
content: The full phrase Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim (بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم) means "In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful." It is the opening verse of every chapter in the Quran (except one) and is recited by Muslims before beginning any meaningful task.

When rendering this full phrase in Arabic calligraphy, longer scripts like Thuluth and Diwani produce the most balanced compositions — their proportions accommodate the extended text more gracefully than compact styles like Kufic. In the generator above, the full phrase is loaded by default so you can immediately compare all 11 styles side by side and pick the one that fits your intended use.

heroImage.alt: Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim (بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم) full phrase in high-resolution Thuluth Arabic calligraphy with ornate gold border on parchment

content: Bismillah (بسم الله) is composed of three Arabic components: bi (with/in), ism (name), and Allah (God). Combined, they mean "In the name of Allah." The extended form, Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim, adds two of Allah's ninety-nine names: al-Rahman (the Most Gracious, whose mercy encompasses all creation) and al-Rahim (the Most Merciful, whose mercy is especially bestowed upon believers).

For Muslims, Bismillah is more than a phrase — it is a spiritual practice. It is recited before eating, before starting a journey, before writing a letter, before entering a home, and before beginning any task worthy of divine blessing. The phrase appears at the beginning of 113 of the 114 chapters (surahs) of the Quran.

Its calligraphic representation has been central to Islamic art for over a thousand years, adorning mosques, manuscripts, ceramics, and textiles from Morocco to Indonesia. This is why Bismillah calligraphy remains one of the most requested designs for both religious and decorative purposes today.

content: After designing your Bismillah calligraphy in the generator above, download it in the format that fits your use case:

PNG — best for web use and social media. Enable transparent background in the generator when you need to layer Bismillah over another image or design.
SVG — best for print, tattoo reference, or wall art since it scales to any size without quality loss.
JPG — smallest file size, good for casual sharing where a solid background is acceptable.
All downloads are free — no watermark, no sign-up, no account required. You can also copy the raw Arabic text of Bismillah directly from the tool for use in documents or social posts.
Bismillah is pronounced "bis-mil-LAH" with the stress on the final syllable. The full phrase, Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim, is pronounced "bis-mil-LAH ar-rah-MAAN ar-ra-HEEM."

In Arabic phonetics, the "H" in Allah and Rahim is a breathy pharyngeal sound (ح), softer than an English "H". The "R" should be lightly rolled. When reciting, take a small pause between "Bismillah" and "ar-Rahman" rather than rushing them together — this small pause is part of traditional recitation etiquette.

faq.question: What does Bismillah mean in Arabic?
faq.answer: Bismillah (بسم الله) means "In the name of Allah" in Arabic. The full phrase Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim (بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم) translates to "In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful." It is recited by Muslims before starting any important task.

faq.question: Which Arabic calligraphy style is best for Bismillah?
faq.answer: Thuluth is the most traditional and revered style for Bismillah calligraphy, historically used in Quranic manuscripts. Diwani offers ornate flowing curves ideal for artistic use. Kufic provides a geometric modern look. Naskh gives a clean readable form suitable for text.

faq.question: Can I download Bismillah calligraphy as PNG or SVG?
faq.answer: Yes. You can download Bismillah in your chosen calligraphy style as a free PNG (with transparent background option), JPG, or SVG vector file. SVG is recommended for print, tattoo reference, or wall art since it scales without quality loss.

faq.question: How do you pronounce Bismillah?
faq.answer: Bismillah is pronounced "bis-mil-LAH" with emphasis on the last syllable. The full phrase Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim is pronounced "bis-mil-LAH ar-rah-MAAN ar-ra-HEEM."

faq.question: Is it appropriate to use Bismillah calligraphy for tattoos or wall art?
faq.answer: Bismillah is a sacred Islamic phrase and its use should be treated with respect. Many Muslims display Bismillah as wall art or on greeting cards. For tattoos, scholarly opinion varies; consult a knowledgeable person from your tradition before proceeding. Always verify the Arabic script with a native speaker.
